metadata.source_root_policy: Paths are relative to the MathDevMCP checkout root. Sibling repositories use ../ segments and are not public/CI fixtures.
metadata.execution_policy: Executable probes are bounded diagnostics for the current high-level workflow layer. They are not full proofs of the source obligations.
non_claims: This manifest is not benchmark-gate evidence.
This manifest is not a public redistributable fixture pack.
A passing executable probe does not prove the full source derivation.
Adapter-required source obligations are not passed or failed by probe status.
No release readiness, external benchmark validity, scientific validity, or broad theorem-proving ability is concluded.

environment_contract.python: current local Python used by MathDevMCP tests
required_runtime: sympy available through the current test environment
environment_contract.optional_backend_policy: Unavailable optional backends are diagnostic and must not be treated as refutations.

cases.title: IFT gradient-bias sign consistency

source_obligation.question: Can we prove the stated IFT gradient-bias formula from the theorem definitions, or is there a sign inconsistency?
required_evidence: derive algebra from definitions of IFT gradient, FD gradient, and adjoint convention
identify the first inconsistent sign boundary
preserve the distinction between theorem-local sign algebra and practical HMC conclusions

executable_probe.faithfulness: The scalar sign-flip probe exercises the same local sign-inconsistency pattern as the source obligation.
executable_probe.does_not_prove: It does not prove the full DSGE theorem, assess solver residuals, or validate/invalidate HMC conclusions.

needed: LaTeX equation extraction for the theorem/proof block

forbidden_claims: The whole DSGE note is false.
The HMC practical conclusion is invalid.
The executable probe proves the source theorem.

cases.title: Kalman prediction-error log-likelihood

source_obligation.question: Can we derive the prediction-error log-likelihood from the Kalman innovations?
required_evidence: use Gaussian log density for each innovation
state positive-definite selected innovation covariance and mask policy

executable_probe.faithfulness: The probe checks the domain assumptions for the log-determinant and solve-form quadratic terms that appear in the Kalman likelihood.
executable_probe.does_not_prove: It does not derive the full likelihood, validate masking semantics, or validate a Kalman implementation.

needed: source-aware derivation packet from innovation distribution

forbidden_claims: The score or Hessian is validated by this likelihood probe.
The executable probe proves the source likelihood derivation.

cases.title: Joseph covariance update equivalence

source_files.role: compact and Joseph covariance update forms

source_obligation.question: Can we prove that the Joseph covariance update is algebraically equivalent to the compact Kalman covariance update under the standard gain definition?
required_evidence: use the standard Kalman gain and innovation covariance relation

executable_probe.faithfulness: The scalar probe checks a small algebraic cancellation used in covariance-update equivalence arguments.
executable_probe.does_not_prove: It does not prove the full matrix Joseph equivalence, positive semidefiniteness under rounding, or backend implementation correctness.

forbidden_claims: The compact form preserves PSD under rounding.
A particular backend implements Joseph form correctly.
The scalar executable probe proves full matrix equivalence.

cases.title: Affine pricing master recursion

source_obligation.question: Can I derive the affine recursion coefficients A_n and B_n from the exponential-affine ansatz and Gaussian transition law?

executable_probe.faithfulness: The probe checks that the current high-level layer refuses to over-certify a matrix-looking affine collection step it cannot safely route.
executable_probe.does_not_prove: It does not derive the affine recursion, apply the Gaussian MGF, or validate any asset-pricing approximation.

forbidden_claims: Every later non-affine approximation is exact.
Empirical pricing validity or identification follows.
The inconclusive executable probe is a failed source derivation.

cases.title: Kalman score same-scalar derivative contract

source_files.role: first-order Kalman score and solve-form score

source_obligation.question: Can I derive the solve-form Kalman score contribution from the prediction-error log-likelihood, and what must be true for it to be a valid HMC gradient?
required_evidence: differentiate log determinant and quadratic form
substitute solve variable S_t w_t = v_t

executable_probe.faithfulness: The probe checks assumptions required by the logdet, solve, and gradient terms in the same-scalar score contract.
executable_probe.does_not_prove: It does not derive the score recursion, prove same-scalar HMC validity, validate Hessian readiness, or establish sampler convergence.

needed: derivative-rule support for solve-form Kalman score
line-linked review packet for HMC gradient validity

forbidden_claims: Posterior validity or sampler convergence follows.
Backend correctness follows from the assumption probe.
